title: Purely Adventurous Turkish Cacao Su Boregi
description: A Jain-friendly Turkish dish featuring cacao and lacquer-glaze, this su boregi is salty, light, and perfect for adventurous eaters. It combines the richness of cacao with the crunch of lacquer-glaze in a unique and exciting way.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the phyllo dough and cut it into squares.
Mix the cacao powder, lacquer-glaze, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl.
Brush the phyllo dough squares with olive oil and sprinkle with the cacao mixture.
Top each square with chopped parsley and scallions.
Fold the phyllo dough squares into triangles and press the edges to seal.
Place the su boregi on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops with olive oil and bake for 20 minutes.
